sources.title: Notion AI product page
sources.excerpt: Notion AI is positioned inside a connected workspace where teams write, summarize, search, and manage wiki knowledge across projects.

sources.title: Notion pricing page
sources.excerpt: Notion offers free and paid plans, with AI features commonly sold as an add-on or included in higher paid workspace plans.

sources.title: Mem product page
sources.excerpt: Mem focuses on personal knowledge capture, AI search, and automatic organization for individuals who want fast recall.

sources.title: Mem AI notes positioning
sources.excerpt: The product emphasizes AI-powered search and resurfacing information from notes without requiring heavy manual folders.

sources.title: Reflect notes product page
sources.excerpt: Reflect is a networked note-taking app with backlinks, daily notes, personal knowledge graphs, AI writing, and fast capture.

sources.title: Reflect pricing
sources.excerpt: Reflect uses a subscription model and targets individual knowledge workers who value privacy and connected notes.

sources.title: Evernote AI features
sources.excerpt: Evernote has a large legacy notes user base and adds AI search, cleanup, and note organization features across devices.

sources.title: Evernote market perception
sources.excerpt: Evernote competes in a crowded note-taking market where users compare AI search, capture speed, syncing, and pricing.
